summary: Scorching Scarlet>Crazy Fingers and tight mid‑set jams
blurb: The second set kicks off with a fiery Estimated Prophet that rolls straight into a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Crazy Fingers sequence. Brent’s keyboards blaze on I Will Take You Home while the band delivers an expansive post‑Space jam before a heartfelt a‑cappella encore.
review: April 3, 1990 at The Omni finds the Dead in top form during what many fans call a “late‑tour firestorm.”  The first set opens strong with Shakedown Street and Hell In A Bucket, but it’s the second set that truly shines: an energetic Estimated Prophet launches into a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Crazy Fingers pairing, followed by a marathon Playing In The Band > Drums > Space stretch that culminates in I Will Take You Home and a soaring Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad.  Brent Mydland’s keyboards are on fire throughout, delivering both blistering solos and a beautiful vocal on his own song, while Jerry’s guitar is “smoking” with sweet phrasing on Row Jimmy and razor‑sharp leads.  Phil’s bass locks in like “peanut butter on jelly,” Mickey’s space drumming ranks among the band’s wildest, and Billy provides rock‑steady support.  The a‑cappella rendition of And We Bid You Good Night caps an evening that feels both tight and wildly exploratory – a must‑listen for anyone chasing that late‑’90 spark.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of eight recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Crazy Fingers sequence, Brent’s keyboards, and Jerry’s guitar. Mixed comments focus on recording quirks rather than performance flaws, so a robust positive sentiment adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard capture with crisp, well‑balanced mix; preserves the tight playing and dynamic nuances despite minor first‑set muddiness.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Crazy Fingers sequence that drives the second set.
Powerful Estimated Prophet opening the second set leading into extended jams.
Brent Mydland’s fiery keyboards and vocal on I Will Take You Home.
25‑minute post‑Space jam culminating in an a‑cappella And We Bid You Good Night.
song_highlights: And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: Smoking guitar tone with sweet phrasing on Row Jimmy and tight lead work throughout; consistently praised as a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Phil: Locked‑in, fluid bass described as "peanut butter on jelly," providing a solid foundation for the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar; noted as energetic on Hell In A Bucket despite one comment calling a Bobby tune uninspired.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards on fire – “tickling the ivories” and delivering powerful solos; vocal performance on I Will Take You Home highlighted as a standout moment.
band_performance.Mickey: Space drumming hailed as among the best ever, creating "crazy drum circles" that drive the improvisational sections.
band_performance.Billy: Steady, supportive drumming that meshes with Mickey to form a robust rhythmic backbone.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
And We Bid You Good Night
